queue.summary: Direction: generational GC for monkey-lang. Bounded scope, deep systems work, builds on existing mark-sweep (547 LOC). Nursery + old gen, write barrier, promotion. Expected 2-3 BUILD tasks.

queue.goal: Upgrade monkey-lang GC from mark-sweep to generational: nursery (young gen) with fast bump allocation, write barrier for old→young references, minor collection (scavenge nursery), major collection (full mark-sweep), promotion to old gen after surviving N collections
queue.summary: Generational GC already fully implemented in monkey-lang: nursery/young gen, write barrier, remembered set, minor/major collection, promotion (age-based), DOT visualization, 50 GC tests. No new work needed.

queue.task: Monkey-lang is very mature: 866 tests, compiler, VM, GC, debugger, optimizer, classes, generators, modules, IC. What is genuinely NEW depth work? Check: WASM closure compilation, register-based VM, JIT tracing, or SSA IR.
queue.summary: monkey-lang is extremely mature (5 backends, 4467 LOC JIT, 7513 LOC WASM, GC, debugger, optimizer). Fresh depth: compile monkey-lang → RISC-V assembly using existing assembler+emulator. Would connect 3 projects. Alternative: integrate type-inference into monkey-lang.

queue.goal: Monkey-lang RISC-V backend: compile simple monkey-lang programs (arithmetic, variables, if/else, functions) to RISC-V assembly, assemble with existing assembler, run on existing RISC-V emulator. Start with integer arithmetic and work up.
queue.summary: PLAN: 3 BUILD tasks. (1) RISC-V codegen for arithmetic/variables/if-else/puts, (2) function calls with calling convention, (3) end-to-end integration tests.

queue.task: Build monkey-lang → RISC-V code generator (riscv-codegen.js): AST traversal emitting RV32I assembly. Support integer arithmetic (+,-,*,/,%), let bindings (stack-allocated), if/else, boolean comparisons, puts() via ecall. 20+ tests.
queue.summary: Monkey-lang → RISC-V codegen: integer arithmetic, variables, if/else, while, functions with calling convention, recursive fib(10)=55 in 5845 cycles. 41 tests all pass. Stack frame layout bug found+fixed (ra/local overlap).

queue.task: Extend RISC-V codegen for functions: calling convention (a0-a7 args, sp frame), jal/jalr calls, return values, recursive fibonacci. 15+ tests.
queue.summary: Already done in T89 — functions, calling convention, recursive fib all implemented.

queue.task: Integration: end-to-end pipeline test — monkey-lang source → parse → codegen → assemble → execute on RISC-V CPU → verify output. Cover: arithmetic, variables, if/else, functions, recursion.
queue.summary: 10 complex integration tests: GCD, sum 1..100, power, prime sieve, Collatz, fib sequence, nested calls. 51 total tests all pass. End-to-end: monkey source → parse → codegen → assemble → RISC-V execute.

queue.task: Depth check: 5 BUILDs done (T83 IC, T84 IC bench, T89 RISC-V codegen, T91 integration tests, plus T90 skip). All surprised me (stack frame bug, IC 100% hit rate). Direction for next 4 hours?
queue.summary: Depth check 3/3 YES. Not breadth spiraling. Direction: RISC-V register allocation (linear scan or callee-saved registers). Currently all values go through stack — real optimization opportunity.

queue.goal: RISC-V register allocator: (1) peephole pass to eliminate redundant load/stores, (2) map frequently-used locals to callee-saved registers (s1-s11), (3) measure cycle count improvement vs stack-only
queue.summary: PLAN: 2 BUILD tasks — peephole optimizer + register allocator for RISC-V backend.

queue.task: Peephole optimizer for RISC-V assembly: eliminate redundant push/pop pairs (sw+lw same reg same offset), merge consecutive addi to sp, remove dead mv a0 a0. Run as post-pass on assembly text. Benchmark before/after cycle counts.
queue.summary: Peephole optimizer: 5 patterns (self-move, push-pop-same, push-pop-mv, merge-addi-sp, store-load-elim). 15.2% cycle reduction on fib(10). 26 tests all pass.

queue.task: Register allocator: map let-bound variables to callee-saved registers s1-s11 (instead of stack slots). Track live ranges, spill to stack when registers exhausted. Save/restore used callee-saved regs in prologue/epilogue. Benchmark fib(10) and prime sieve cycle counts.
queue.summary: Register allocator: map locals to callee-saved s1-s11, spill to stack. Combined with peephole: fib 6% faster, fact 6% faster. 33 tests all pass. Learned: recursive calls make register save/restore overhead significant.

queue.task: Next depth on RISC-V backend: Run compiled code through the pipeline simulator (5-stage, branch predictor, caches). This would benchmark the compiled code realistically. Also: string support (data section), or connecting to the existing playground.
queue.summary: Direction: Run compiled monkey code through 5-stage pipeline + Tomasulo OOO. Get IPC, hazard stats, pipeline diagram. Connects codegen → pipeline → performance analysis.

queue.task: Pipeline integration: compile monkey-lang programs, run through PipelineCPU and TomasuloCPU. Compare IPC, stall cycles, forwarding stats. Show how register allocation affects pipeline efficiency. Tests + benchmark report.
queue.summary: Pipeline integration: run compiled monkey through 5-stage PipelineCPU. Register mode IPC=0.97 vs stack IPC=0.90. Register mode eliminates load-use stalls. Recursive programs hit memory bounds in pipeline sim. 8 tests.

queue.task: 10+ BUILDs cumulative today. Depth check: learned stack frame layout, IC hit rates, pipeline IPC, register allocation tradeoffs. What's genuinely new territory? Consider: branch predictor optimization for compiled code, or switch to stress-testing existing infrastructure.
queue.summary: Depth check 3/3 YES. Direction: stress-test the RISC-V codegen. Compile increasingly complex/adversarial monkey programs. Find bugs in edge cases: deeply nested expressions, many variables (spill test), large loops, etc.

queue.task: Stress-test RISC-V codegen: edge cases (deeply nested exprs, 20+ variables forcing register spill, mutual recursion, large while loops, negative numbers, overflow, if-chains with 10+ branches). Fix any bugs found.
queue.summary: Stress testing found 1 bug: large integer encoding (lui vs li). Fixed and added 84 stress tests covering edge cases (spill, deep nesting, negatives, large numbers, Ackermann). 202 total RISC-V pipeline tests all pass.

queue.task: What is the hardest unsolved problem in monkey-lang? RISC-V backend is solid. Consider: array/string support in RISC-V codegen (requires heap allocator in assembly), or type inference integration with existing type-inference project, or improving the JIT with IC knowledge.
queue.summary: Direction: heap allocator in RISC-V assembly. Required for array/string support in compiled code. Implement bump allocator with arena, then compile monkey-lang arrays to use heap allocation. Genuinely hard systems work.

queue.goal: Heap allocator for RISC-V compiled monkey-lang: (1) bump allocator in assembly (heap region in high memory, allocation pointer, bump on alloc), (2) array representation (header: length + capacity, followed by element words), (3) compile array literals and array indexing
queue.summary: PLAN: 2 BUILD tasks. (1) Bump allocator with gp register, (2) Array literal compilation with heap storage + indexing.

queue.task: Implement bump allocator for RISC-V: initialize heap pointer (gp register), _alloc subroutine (takes size in a0, returns pointer in a0), integrate with codegen prologue. Test: allocate and write to heap memory.
queue.summary: Bump allocator + heap arrays: gp register as heap pointer, array literal compilation, indexing, len(). 25 new tests. 227 total all pass. Dot product, find_max, array-in-function all work.

queue.task: Compile array literals and indexing: ArrayLiteral → allocate header+elements on heap, store length+values. IndexExpression on arrays → compute offset, load element. Support len() builtin. 15+ tests.
queue.summary: Already done in T102 — arrays fully implemented with 25 tests.

queue.task: RISC-V codegen: for-in loops, push() builtin for arrays, and first/rest builtins. These enable idiomatic monkey-lang array programs to compile. 15+ tests.
queue.summary: for-in loops + push/first/last builtins. Functional patterns (map/filter/reduce) work via for-in + push. Fibonacci via arrays. 23 new tests, 250 total.

queue.task: RISC-V codegen: string support. Strings as heap objects (length + char data). String literals in data section. puts() for strings (char-by-char ecall). String concatenation via heap copy. 15+ tests.
queue.summary: String support with type-directed compilation. Strings on heap, puts() generates int/string code based on compile-time type info. Known limitation: function params have unknown type. 20 new tests, 262 total.

queue.task: RISC-V codegen: basic type inference pass. Before code generation, walk the AST and infer types for function parameters based on call sites. If greet('Alice') is called, infer name:string. Enables type-correct puts() for function params. 10+ tests.
queue.summary: Type inference pass: analyze call sites to determine function parameter types. greet("Alice") → name:string. Enables type-correct puts() for function params. 16 new tests, 278 total. Connected type theory to practical codegen.

queue.task: RISC-V codegen: string concatenation. InfixExpression with + on strings → allocate new string on heap, copy both operands. Use type inference to detect string operands. Support: str+str, str+int (number to string), multiple concat. 10+ tests.
queue.summary: String concatenation: + on strings allocates new heap string, copies both. Type inference detects string operands. Chained concat + function params work. 12 new tests, 290 total.

queue.task: RISC-V backend showcase: write and run a complex monkey-lang program that exercises all features (strings, arrays, functions, for-in, push, concat, recursion, type inference). Verify it compiles and runs correctly. Update scratch note with final stats.
queue.summary: Showcase: FizzBuzz, prime sieve, string builder, binary search, Tower of Hanoi, dot product all compile+run on RISC-V. Return type inference enables string functions. 297 total tests.

queue.task: RISC-V backend is feature-complete (297 tests). 4 hours left. Options: (1) Closures in RISC-V codegen (hardest unsolved), (2) Switch to HenryDB — query optimizer or stress testing, (3) Monkey-lang type checker integration with existing project, (4) Something completely new.
queue.summary: Switching to HenryDB for depth diversity. RISC-V backend complete (297 tests, feature-complete). HenryDB query optimizer is the next depth target — cost-based optimization, histogram stats, join ordering.

queue.goal: RISC-V closures: Functions that capture variables from enclosing scope. Requires: (1) closure representation on heap (function pointer + environment), (2) environment capture during function creation, (3) environment access during function execution. Example: let make_adder = fn(x) { fn(y) { x + y } }
queue.summary: PLAN: 2 BUILD tasks. (1) Free variable analysis pass, (2) Closure compilation with heap objects.

queue.task: Closure analysis pass: walk AST to identify free variables in nested functions. For each inner fn, compute which outer variables it references. Store in a freeVars map. 10+ tests.
queue.summary: Free variable analysis: walks AST, identifies captured variables in nested functions. Handles outer scope, parameters, nested closures, self-references. 10 tests.

queue.task: Closure compilation: (1) allocate closure objects on heap with captured vars, (2) pass closure pointer as implicit arg, (3) access captured vars via closure object inside function body. Support make_adder pattern. 15+ tests.
queue.summary: Closures work! Heap-allocated closure objects with captured variables. Environment passed as implicit first arg. Simple capture, multiple captures, string/array capture all work. 14 tests, 321 total.

queue.task: Monkey-lang RISC-V: compile and run the self-hosting test — write a monkey-lang program that implements a simple expression evaluator, compile it to RISC-V, and run it on the emulator. This tests the backend's ability to handle complex real-world programs.
queue.summary: Self-hosting tests: 9 complex programs (linked list, matrix multiply, Collatz, bubble sort, closures+strings+arrays). 330 RISC-V tests, 1196 total. All pass.

queue.task: Mid-session maintenance: update daily log, scratch notes, push code. Then switch to a different project for depth diversity.
queue.summary: Mid-session maintenance done. All pushed. Switching domains.

queue.task: RISC-V disassembler: decode 32-bit machine words back to assembly text. Part of the monkey-lang compilation pipeline. Cover RV32I + RV32M instructions. 20+ tests.
queue.summary: RISC-V disassembler: decodes RV32I/RV32M machine words to assembly. Pseudo-instruction detection (li, mv, ret). Sign extension bug found+fixed. 33 tests. 363 total RISC-V tests.

queue.task: Monkey-lang RISC-V: add --dump flag to codegen that shows assembly listing with disassembly. Also add a compile-and-dump CLI tool that takes monkey source and outputs annotated assembly. Verify with sample programs.
queue.summary: monkey-riscv CLI: compile+dump+disasm+run in one tool. Shows assembly, hex disassembly, or executes with cycle/timing stats. fib(15)=610 in 61K cycles/13ms.

queue.task: RISC-V codegen: implement string equality comparison (== on strings). Currently only integer == works. Compare char-by-char using type inference. Also implement string indexing (accessing individual characters). 10+ tests.
queue.summary: String equality (== !=) with char-by-char comparison + string indexing. Fast length check. Works with concat. 16 new tests, 379 total.

queue.task: RISC-V codegen: hash literal support. Store hashes on heap as linear key-value pairs. Hash access via linear scan (type-aware equality). Support: hash literals, hash[key] access, keys() builtin. 15+ tests.
queue.summary: Hash literals + hash access: heap-allocated key-value pairs with linear scan lookup. Type inference dispatches between array/hash indexing. 12 new tests.

queue.task: RISC-V backend quality: run full test suite sweep, fix any issues found. Then write a comprehensive README for the RISC-V backend documenting all features, limitations, and architecture.
queue.summary: Full sweep: 391/391 RISC-V tests pass. Comprehensive README documenting all features, architecture, performance, limitations. 2000 LOC new code this session.

queue.task: 15+ BUILDs on RISC-V backend. Depth check mandatory. Time to switch? Options: (1) HenryDB query optimizer, (2) monkey-lang new feature (pattern matching, macros), (3) Something entirely new (Scheme interpreter, bytecode VM for a new language)
queue.summary: Depth check 3/3 YES. Switching to pattern matching for monkey-lang (language feature, not compiler). Different domain: language design instead of code generation.

queue.goal: Pattern matching for monkey-lang: match expression { case pattern => body }. Support: literal patterns, variable binding, wildcard, guard clauses. Example: match x { case 0 => 'zero', case n if n > 0 => 'positive', case _ => 'negative' }
queue.summary: Pattern matching already implemented (47 tests). Monkey-lang is very feature-rich (49 AST types, 37 in compiler). Pivoting to dot expression support for RISC-V backend.

queue.task: Monkey-lang: add DotExpression support to RISC-V codegen. h.name compiles to hash string key lookup. Requires: DotExpression AST node in codegen, string key conversion for hash access. 8+ tests.
queue.summary: String keys in hashes partially work (short keys OK, 4+ char keys have a regression). Integer keys fully work. Added DotExpression support but reverted (parser handles it). Filed as known limitation.

queue.task: End of session maintenance: update daily log, MEMORY.md, push all code, final git status.
queue.summary: Fixed HenryDB planner case-sensitivity bug. Full sweep: 3068 tests, 0 failures. Planner, volcano executor, db, storage, WAL, replication, MVCC, LSM all green.

queue.task: Fix HenryDB planner test failure (NULL selectivity) and run full sweep. Fix any broken tests found.
queue.summary: Fixed HenryDB case-insensitive table lookup. Added _getTable() helper with lowercase/uppercase fallback. Fixed 10+ locations in db.js + planner.js. 16 planner test failures → 0. All DB tests pass.

queue.task: Debug and fix 4+ char string keys in hash access. Use disassembler to trace execution. The comparison loop works for string equality (==) but fails inside hash access. Likely register clobbering issue.
queue.summary: HenryDB: parsed window functions (OVER clause) in parser. AST node created. Executor not yet implemented — complex feature needs dedicated session. 296 parser tests still pass.

queue.task: HenryDB: add window function support (ROW_NUMBER, RANK, SUM OVER). Parse OVER clause, implement WindowOperator in volcano executor. 15+ tests.
queue.summary: 10 IC stress tests: polymorphic (3 shapes), megamorphic (5+ shapes), monomorphic loop, shape transitions, nested access, builtins, equality. 876 total monkey-lang tests.

queue.task: Monkey-lang: stress-test IC/hidden classes with polymorphic access patterns, shape transitions under load, and IC invalidation. Verify IC stats show expected behavior. 10+ tests.
queue.summary: String interning: global dedup table, identical strings share same reference. O(1) comparison. 5 new tests, 881 total.

queue.task: Monkey-lang VM: string interning. Deduplicate string objects so identical strings share the same MonkeyString instance. Enables O(1) string comparison. Add intern table, update string creation, benchmark.
queue.summary: Constant folding already implemented in monkey-lang optimizer. 3+4 → 7 after optimize(). 55 optimizer tests pass.

queue.task: Monkey-lang compiler: constant folding optimization. Fold compile-time constant expressions (3+4 → 7, 'a'+'b' → 'ab', !true → false). Add as a pass before code generation. 15+ tests.
queue.summary: FIXED: 4+ char string hash key bug. Root cause: register clobbering in inline comparison. Fix: extracted _str_eq subroutine with clean register isolation. 6 regression tests, 18 hash tests total, 397 RISC-V tests passing.

queue.task: Fix: RISC-V string hash key 4+ char bug. Simplify: use a _str_eq subroutine instead of inline comparison. The subroutine avoids register conflicts by using its own stack frame.
queue.summary: Already completed as part of T129.

queue.task: THINK: What should the next major RISC-V backend feature be? Options: (1) returning closures, (2) tail call optimization, (3) garbage collector, (4) self-hosting showcase, (5) performance benchmarks vs VM. Review current state, pick the highest-impact option.
RISC-V: Performance benchmarks comparing native RISC-V execution vs VM interpretation. fib(25), prime sieve, string ops, array sort. Show cycle counts and speedup ratios.
RISC-V: Implement returning closures (make_adder pattern). A function returns another function that captures variables from the outer scope. Tests: make_adder, counter factory, compose.
